name: Ronneberger et al., 'U-Net', MICCAI 2015

task.description: Create a self-contained 1920x1080 HTML slide titled 'U-Net (Ronneberger et al., 2015)'. LEFT two-thirds (66%): an inline-SVG U-shaped architecture. The contracting path descends through 5 levels of feature-map boxes, EACH labeled with its channel count inside the box (1 -> 64 -> 128 -> 256 -> 512 -> 1024 at the bottleneck), with down-arrows labeled 'max pool 2x2'; the expanding path ascends through the mirror levels labeled (512 -> 256 -> 128 -> 64) with up-arrows labeled 'up-conv 2x2'. Horizontal dashed 'copy & crop' skip arrows link each encoder level to the SAME-HEIGHT decoder level. RIGHT third (34%): a dense panel containing (a) a 5-row resolution table 'level 1: 572x572 / level 2: 284x284 / level 3: 140x140 / level 4: 68x68 / level 5 (bottleneck): 32x32', (b) five bullets ('Fully convolutional, no dense layers', 'Skip connections concatenate encoder features', 'Heavy elastic augmentation', 'Overlap-tile for large images', 'Won ISBI 2015 cell-tracking'), and (c) a one-line note 'Output: 388x388 segmentation map'. Footnote at bottom.
requirements: Every feature-map box shows its channel-count label fully inside the box (none clipped), for all encoder and decoder levels
Each of the four 'copy & crop' skip arrows links an encoder level to the decoder level at the SAME vertical height (horizontal, level-aligned)
The contracting and expanding arms are vertically symmetric: corresponding levels share the same top edge
No feature-map box overlaps another; no skip arrow passes through a box label
The down-arrow ('max pool 2x2') and up-arrow ('up-conv 2x2') labels are all fully visible and not clipped
The right panel's 5 resolution rows, 5 bullets and the output note are all present and fully inside the panel
The diagram (left 66%) and the right panel do not overlap
Nothing overflows the 1920x1080 canvas; no scrollbar
Each bullet is at most two lines
Every text element is at least 11px

ss_html:
U-Net (Ronneberger et al., 2015)

Resolution Levels
Level 1: 572×572
Level 2: 284×284
Level 3: 140×140
Level 4: 68×68
Level 5 (bottleneck): 32×32
Key Features
  • Fully convolutional, no dense layers
  • Skip connections concatenate encoder features
  • Heavy elastic augmentation
  • Overlap-tile for large images
  • Won ISBI 2015 cell-tracking
Output: 388×388 segmentation map
Seminal architecture for biomedical image segmentation • Encoder-decoder with skip connections
rv_html:
U-Net (Ronneberger et al., 2015)

Resolution Levels

Level 1: 572×572
Level 2: 284×284
Level 3: 140×140
Level 4: 68×68
Level 5 (bottleneck): 32×32

Key Features

  • Fully convolutional, no dense layers
  • Skip connections concatenate encoder features
  • Heavy elastic augmentation
  • Overlap-tile for large images
  • Won ISBI 2015 cell-tracking
Output: 388×388 segmentation map
Biomedical image segmentation architecture with symmetric encoder-decoder structure

trace.feedback:
Quality score: 70%
DOES NOT MEET REQUIREMENTS

Issues found:
  1. [MAJOR] The skip connections (copy & crop arrows) are not horizontally aligned - encoder and decoder levels at the same vertical position don't align properly (at center)
  2. [MAJOR] The contracting and expanding arms are not vertically symmetric - corresponding encoder and decoder levels don't share the same top edge (at center)
  3. [MINOR] Some down-arrow and up-arrow labels appear to have inconsistent positioning and may be partially clipped (at center)

Suggested fixes:
  1. Align encoder and decoder levels horizontally so skip connections are truly horizontal
  2. Ensure corresponding encoder/decoder levels share the same vertical position for symmetry
  3. Verify all arrow labels are fully visible and consistently positioned

  2. [CRITICAL] RECURRED: The contracting and expanding arms are not vertically symmetric - corresponding encoder and decoder levels don't share the same top edge alignment (at center)

Let me redesign the layout with a proper grid system: The SVG viewBox is 1230 x 960. I need 5 levels for encoder + bottleneck + 4 levels for decoder. Now I'm refining the horizontal layout to fit the 960px SVG height better, using 120px width for encoder/decoder boxes and 140px for the bottleneck, then spacing them across the canvas with the encoder layers on the left, bottleneck in the middle, and decoder layers mirrored on the right. Positioning the vertical levels so there's a 72px gap between the bottom of level 4 and the top of the bottleneck, which gives good visual separation. Now I'm realizing the key constraint: in a proper U-Net diagram, the encoder cascades diagonally down-right while the decoder goes up-right, but for skip connections to work horizontally between corresponding levels, the encoder and decoder boxes at each level need to share the same y-coordinate. So I need to position the encoder staircase going down the left side and the decoder staircase going up the right side, keeping matching levels aligned vertically.
